description: Evaluates automation, orchestration, and integration maturity—service governance, SOAR platform and orchestration, security playbook development, automated remediation workflows, cross-platform integration and vendor lock-in risk, and AI-assisted security automation governance.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: An automation, orchestration, and integration service exists, is scoped appropriately, and is reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy.
evaluation_focus: Enterprise automation and orchestration service exists
Regular automation service efficacy review

capabilities.name: SOAR platform and orchestration
capabilities.description: A security orchestration, automation, and response platform coordinates workflows across security tools, supports enterprise-wide coverage, and is reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: SOAR or equivalent orchestration platform adoption
Enterprise-wide orchestration coverage
Cross-tool workflow coordination
Regular SOAR platform configuration review

capabilities.name: Security playbook development and maintenance
capabilities.description: Documented security playbooks cover common incident and remediation scenarios, are version-controlled, tested regularly, and reviewed at least quarterly for accuracy.
evaluation_focus: Documented security playbooks for common scenarios
Playbook version control and change management
Regular playbook testing and tabletop exercises
Regular playbook accuracy and coverage review

capabilities.name: Automated remediation and response workflows
capabilities.description: Automated workflows execute approved playbooks for containment and remediation with human-in-the-loop gates for high-risk actions and quarterly effectiveness review.
evaluation_focus: Automated containment and remediation workflows
Human-in-the-loop approval for high-risk actions
Workflow integration with ticketing and incident management
Regular automated workflow effectiveness review

capabilities.name: Cross-platform integration and vendor lock-in risk
capabilities.description: Security automation integrates across endpoints, cloud, identity, and network platforms; vendor dependency is assessed and lock-in is justified or mitigated.
evaluation_focus: Cross-platform security tool integration
Vendor dependency assessment across endpoints, devices, and applications
Evaluation and justification or mitigation of vendor lock-in

capabilities.name: AI-assisted security automation governance
capabilities.description: Policies govern AI use in security automation and playbook execution, with analyst oversight and quarterly review of AI-driven action accuracy.
evaluation_focus: Governance for AI use in security automation workflows
Analyst oversight of AI-recommended playbook actions
Regular review of AI-driven automation accuracy

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
